Press & creator kits

3Cats Studio — press.

Kits for our published titles — fact sheets, copy blocks, spoiler-safe screenshots, and permission to cover. Everything here is cleared: record, stream, and monetize freely.

All Time High · Aug 19 Dear Nobody · Sep 23

Title 01 · out Wed Aug 19, 2026

All Time High

A moral-descent finance thriller told through your phone, your feeds, and a live trading terminal. Cleared for coverage — record, stream, and monetize freely.

Fact sheet

at a glance
TitleAll Time High
Developer / Publisher3Cats Studio
Based inNetherlands

PlatformsWindows · macOS · SteamOS / Steam Deck
Price$5.99 USD (Steam)

Website3cats.studio
Press contactinfo@3cats.studio

The hook

lead with this
One-liner

How far would you go in a mania? Every choice has a ledger. Every gain has a price.

A live candlestick market (IDX / SAFE / $FROTH / $VELA) inside a narrative game — the finance-thriller differentiator. Every capsule, screenshot, and clip leads with the terminal.

Copy blocks

ready to quote
Short description (tweetable)

A moral-descent thriller told through your phone, your feeds, and a trading terminal. Chase a California fortune one “just one more trade” at a time — through a crypto mania where every gain has a price. Six endings. How far will you go?

Description (long)

All Time High is a text-based thriller about the hunger to get rich. You install home theaters in hillside mansions and watch other people’s money grow on the wall — until, with $200 of grocery money, you buy your first coin and feel the first hit. A boutique fund notices you. A memecoin turns a joke into a fortune. A private token offers you the inside of a room you were only ever hired to wire.

You read the story on the surfaces where the mania actually happens: a group chat, a news feed, a live trading terminal, a bank notification at 2 a.m. There’s no combat and no map — just choices, consequences, and a number that keeps climbing until it doesn’t. Four hidden variables quietly track who you’re becoming, and six distinct endings each name what it cost.

Features
  • A story told entirely through the interface — messages, feeds, email, and a market terminal you actually trade.
  • Choices with a ledger: hidden cash / greed / integrity / heat track your descent. The story remembers.
  • A scripted market you trade — buy/sell moments move your net worth and bend the story.
  • Six endings — clean exit, exile, a federal knock, the fall guy, the whistleblower, rock bottom.
  • Adaptive music that shifts with the market and the tension, layer by layer.
  • A finishable one-shot (2.5–3.5 h), built to replay for the endings you didn’t take.
  • Fully offline, single-player. 10 languages.
For fans of

STONKS-9800 · Emily is Away · A Dark Room · Slay the Princess — story-rich, choices-matter interactive fiction where the interface is the game.

Title 02 · out Wed Sep 23, 2026

Dear Nobody

An epistolary romance × the uncanny, played at a wartime censor’s desk. Cleared for coverage — record, stream, and monetize freely.

Fact sheet

at a glance
TitleDear Nobody
Developer / Publisher3Cats Studio
Based inNetherlands
ReleaseWed Sep 23, 2026
PlatformsWindows · macOS · Linux
Price$5.99 USD
LanguagesEnglish · Türkçe · 简体中文 · 한국어 · 日本語 · Deutsch · Español (LatAm) · Français · Русский · Português-BR (10)
Length2–4 h · one-shot novella · 10 chapters · 6 endings
DemoNo public demo — press & creators get full embargo keys on request
Storestore.steampowered.com/app/4957320
Website3cats.studio
Press contactinfo@3cats.studio

The hook

lead with this
One-liner

Every word you strike is struck from the relationship itself.

The censor’s desk IS the interface — read, redact, stamp. The most intimate choices are the ones you cross out. And the correspondence has begun, impossibly, to answer your pen.

Copy blocks

ready to quote
Short description (tweetable)

You are a censor in a nameless war, blacking out the letters of two lovers who have never met. Their whole romance lives on paper — so every word you strike is struck from the love itself. Then the letters begin, impossibly, to answer your pen. A melancholy epistolary romance. Six endings.

Description (long)

You are a woman at a censor’s desk, far behind a nameless front. Across your desk pass the letters of two strangers — pen-pals who have never once met, who know each other only through the words they send. You hold a stamp, a pen, and a book of regulations, and it is your work to strike out anything a soldier must not know.

But these two have nothing but the letters. Cross out a line and it is gone from their love, not just from the page. Then a letter answers a question you only asked with your pen; a line you struck weeks ago returns, uncrossed, in another hand; and one thread stops writing to the front and starts writing to you. The game never says whether the paper is haunted or whether you are simply coming apart at your own quiet desk. You decide what you believe.

Features
  • A redaction mechanic you feel in your chest — censorship as a love language, and a weapon. The most intimate choices are the ones you cross out.
  • Six endings drawn from two fates at once — what becomes of the lovers, and what becomes of you.
  • A quietly uncanny story that never explains itself: realistic on the surface, wrong underneath.
  • A copybook — save a single line from a letter before it leaves your hands forever; some come back to matter.
  • A living document, not a menu — the paper cools and the lamp dims as the story darkens.
  • Fully offline, single-player. A quiet night or two (2–4 h). 10 languages.
For fans of

Slay the Princess · Scarlet Hollow · Roadwarden · Papers, Please — story-rich, choices-matter interactive fiction where the interface is the game.

Screenshots

spoiler-safe

Full asset pack — capsules, screenshots, wordmark, and the hero clip — available on request. Email info@3cats.studio for the media kit and an embargo key. Store capsules and screenshots are also public on the Steam page.

Permission to use

creators & press

Creators and press are welcome to record, stream, and monetize videos of All Time High and Dear Nobody (and their demos or embargo builds). Screenshots and capsule art in these kits may be used in coverage.

Both games are works of fiction; any resemblance to real people, assets, companies, wars, or nations is coincidental. All Time High is not financial advice.
